diagrams.title: Hierarchical Directory Structure
diagrams.description: Tree-like directory organization with root and nested subdirectories
diagrams.purpose: Understanding tree-based file organization and parent-child relationships in modern operating systems

diagrams.title: Disk Scheduling Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison showing head movement for FCFS, SSTF, and SCAN algorithms
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how different algorithms affect disk head movement and seek time optimization

diagrams.title: File Allocation Methods
diagrams.description: Three main methods showing how disk blocks are allocated to files
elements: Indexed: index block pointing to data blocks 25,28,31,35
diagrams.purpose: Understanding differences between contiguous, linked, and indexed file allocation strategies

diagrams.title: Disk Geometry Components
diagrams.description: Physical structure of a hard disk showing tracks, sectors, and cylinders
diagrams.purpose: Understanding physical organization of data on magnetic disks and cylinder concept
